Discord vs Lark: Which Is Better?

Choosing between Discord and Lark? Here's a side-by-side comparison to help you decide.

Quick verdict: Choose Discord if you need communities or gamers. Choose Lark if you prioritize remote-teams or startups.

Discord

PricingFree / $9.99/mo Nitro
Rating★★★★ 4.4/5

Key Features

  • Voice channels with crystal-clear, low-latency audio
  • Server organization with roles, categories, and granular permissions
  • Text channels with markdown, embeds, and thread support
  • Comprehensive bot API for moderation, automation, and engagement
  • Stage channels for audio-based events and presentations
  • Screen sharing and video calls with up to 25 participants

Pros

  • Free tier is incredibly generous — unlimited messages, history storage, and 25MB file uploads
  • Voice chat quality is excellent; built on Opus codec, low latency, and handles 25+ people without issues
  • Server structure with roles and permissions lets you build complex, tiered communities
  • Bot ecosystem is massive and enables everything from moderation to music to mini-games
  • Stage channels (audio-only events) are great for AMAs, town halls, and community calls

Cons

  • Not designed for formal business communication — threading is limited and message formatting is basic
  • No built-in task management, project tracking, or productivity features
  • Can get noisy fast; a busy server with multiple active channels requires active moderation
  • Nitro's benefits (higher file uploads, custom emojis) are mostly cosmetic for business use
  • Screen sharing quality drops noticeably at higher resolutions compared to Teams or Zoom
Try Discord →

Lark

PricingFree / $12/mo Pro / custom Enterprise
Rating★★★★ 4/5

Key Features

  • All-in-one suite with messenger, docs, calendar, video, and sheets
  • Video conferencing with 100+ participants on free tier
  • Smart docs with real-time collaboration and sidebar chat
  • Calendar with scheduling automation and cross-platform sync
  • Messenger with threaded conversations, bots, and app integrations
  • Cloud storage with 100GB on free tier

Pros

  • The free tier is almost unbelievable — 100+ participant video calls, 100GB storage, unlimited messaging
  • All-in-one means you don't need separate apps for chat, docs, calendar, and video
  • Messenger integrates deeply with docs; you can co-edit a document while chatting about it in the sidebar
  • Video conferencing quality is reliable and includes features like background blur, recording, and live captions
  • Sheets are surprisingly capable for a free alternative to Google Sheets

Cons

  • Privacy concerns because it's owned by ByteDance (TikTok's parent) — a non-starter for some organizations
  • Less adopted in Western markets, which means fewer community templates and integrations
  • The all-in-one approach can feel overwhelming; there's a learning curve for the full suite
  • Some integrations with Western tools (Salesforce, HubSpot) are less mature than competitors
  • Occasional UI inconsistencies between the different apps within the suite

Which Should You Choose?

Both Discord and Lark are excellent tools in their category. The right choice depends on your specific needs:

  • Choose Discord if: Discord started as a place for gamers to talk during raids and somehow became a legit team communication platform. It's free, it's feature-rich, and it's where a surprising number of open-source projects, developer communities, and even corporate teams now live. The voice chat quality is genuinely better than most business tools.
  • Choose Lark if: Lark is ByteDance's answer to Google Workspace — an all-in-one suite with messenger, docs, calendar, video, and sheets in a single app. It's huge in Asia and gaining traction elsewhere, largely because the free tier is absurdly generous (video calls with 100+ participants, 100GB cloud storage). The elephant in the room is that it's made by the company behind TikTok.
We may earn a commission if you purchase through links. All opinions are our own.
